scores.head_floor_note: HEAD distance is 149 because the function is held by an asmfix `replace_with_asmfile` bridge with a stub C body; when the bridge is stripped + the cheat-asm injection (371 lines) is stripped, the stub builds to build_insns=2. The 149 measurement is a sandbox-strip artifact, NOT a reflection of how close pure-C could be. The candidate's 52 is the FIRST honest pure-C measurement and is below the prior session's reported 76 floor.

sessions.lever: workflow round 4: tested 3 additional structural levers from the score-52 base; all confirmed the 52 floor as a list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY wall analogous to cpu_side_move_dir_4 sched.c residual
sessions.note: Confirmed candidate floor 52 / build_insns 151 == target 151 reproduces with --keep-cheat-asm. Performed index-aligned objdump diff (tmp/diff_cbb0.py): residual is 66 raw insn diffs concentrated in two regions — big-packet idx 45-91 and small-packet idx 117-139. The DECISIVE diagnostic: target emits `lui $a3,0x3FF` at idx 49 (the 0x03FFFFFF mask LUI) with its paired `ori $a3,$a3,0xFFFF` deferred 36 instructions later to idx 85, right before the `sw $a3,(p187C)` at idx 89. Candidate emits the lui+ori as an adjacent pair at idx 49-50. This is a deliberate list-scheduler split that GCC 2.7.2's combine pass normally keeps as one atomic 2-insn materialization — the candidate's compile takes the natural path, the target's compile somehow split it. New levers tested this session, ALL CONFIRMED 52 FLOOR: (1) named intermediates `u32 ot_hdr = ...; u32 sign_E1 = ((arg1>>31)<<10) | 0xE1000000;` (no change, combine folds single-use locals); (2) constant rewrite `*p187C = ~0xFC000000` (no change, combine canonicalizes); (3) hoisted BF48 deref `s32 ot_link = *D_8009BF48;` at top of big-packet block (REGRESSED 52->65, pushes the load too early in scheduling). The cpu_side_move_dir_4 sessions 5-10 documented this exact class of sched.c INSN_PRIORITY wall with 10 sessions + 16,800+ permuter iterations all plateauing on register-rename diffs at matched insn count. Per [[no-compiler-divergence]] + [[no-new-park-categories]], the chain-extender lever that worked for cpu_side_move_dir_4 in session 5 is FORBIDDEN (2026-06-02); no analogous pure-C lever remains in the explored space. Honest endpoint: 52 floor is genuinely the structural ceiling for this function's pure-C reconstruction. The remaining named avenue (directed permuter from clean offset-0 target) was NOT executed this session due to budget; given cpu_side_move_dir_4 permuter plateau at 16,800 iters on identical-class residual, the prior-art evidence strongly suggests it would also plateau on register-rename diffs at score-class ~52. src/ reverted; verify-oracle SHA1 == oracle (62efab4f...).

sessions.lever: workflow round 5 (this session): tested 2 additional fresh structural levers from the score-52 base targeting the list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY residual specifically; both confirmed the 52 floor
sessions.note: NO_PROGRESS. Re-deployed candidate.c per the documented recipe (extern s32 *D_8009BF48 forward decl included). Re-verified: score 52, build_insns 151, target_insns 151 EXACT, rules_dropped=1, scorable=true — the documented floor reproduces at HEAD 06958fe3. Two fresh levers tested, NEITHER tried in any prior session per rejected_forms ledger: (1) *p187C = 0x03FFFFFF store moved BEFORE D_800F1874 store (rejected_forms only had "after D_800F1878" tested → 54; moving it earlier in source order tested as the symmetric variant) — REGRESSED 52→53, breaks target's store source order without helping the lui/ori scheduling split; (2) constant rewrite `*p187C = ((u32)0x3FFu << 16) | 0xFFFFu;` — explicitly decomposed the 0x03FFFFFF literal into a shift-OR form that mirrors GCC's MIPS lui+ori expansion at the C level, hoping cc1's expand stages would keep them as discrete RTL operations rather than fold to a single movabs. Result: 52 floor unchanged (combine canonicalizes the shift-OR back to the literal before the constant materializer's split-vs-adjacent decision). Cumulative ~32 negative levers across 5 sessions. The list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decision that defers ori-of-0x03FFFFFF to its paired sw consumer (36 insns past the no-dep lui) is genuinely outside the pure-C source space's reachable RTL shapes — combine folds every literal-equivalent constant form, and every chain-extender that would adjust INSN_PRIORITY without emitting bytes is forbidden per cheats-by-any-spelling. The ONE genuinely-untried lever remaining in the named menu is the directed permuter from a clean single-function offset-0 target (next_hypotheses[1]), but cpu_side_move_dir_4's session-10 documented 16,800-iter permuter plateau at clean-target score 1125 (residual: register-rename diffs at matched 179/179 insns) STRONGLY suggests CBB0's permuter from base 52 would plateau identically. src/ reverted; verify-oracle SHA1 == oracle (62efab4f...). ESCALATION CANDIDATE — see next_hypotheses[0].

reviewer.evidence: Not yet invoked on the candidate. The candidate is structural pure-C with no obvious cheat constructs (no register pins, no register-asm, no volatile coercion on globals, no unused locals — the (void)arg2/arg3 is the K&R-style ABI honoring of unused params, see SOTN community standard). Invoke cheat-reviewer before any Match: commit. Manual self-audit notes the candidate's `(void)arg2; (void)arg3;` declarations have semantic purpose (silence unused-param warnings on a function whose ABI takes 4 args but logic uses 2 — the calling convention is what matters for the prologue size, and this preserves it cleanly). [2026-06-03 update] Score-52 candidate body verified reproducible (build_insns 151 == target 151). Reviewer still owes an independent PASS verdict on the candidate.c body before any Match: commit attempt.

remaining_gap.summary: List-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the GPU packet store sequence. Target interleaves the 0x03FFFFFF constant materialization (lui $a3 0x3FFFFFF + ori $a3 0xFFFF split by ~20 insns) with the BF48 load chain; mine emits them adjacent. Same class as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec sched.c walls. NO frame coercion, NO register allocation diffs — pure scheduling.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD's single rule is `func_8007CBB0: replace_with_asmfile "asm/funcs/func_8007CBB0.s"` — an asmfile bridge over a stub body. The candidate's score-52 form is structurally and semantically correct; to commit, the bridge rule would be retired (drop the bridge → replace stub with candidate body) only if score reaches 0. The structural shape is the right base for the close; the residual is scheduling-only.

next_hypotheses: PARK_CANDIDATE ESCALATION (round 5, 2026-06-04): After 5 sessions + cumulative ~32 negative levers, the 52 floor is empirically confirmed as the structural ceiling for this function's pure-C reconstruction. The decisive residual diagnostic (target splits lui $a3,0x3FF at idx 49 from ori $a3,$a3,0xFFFF at idx 85 by 36 insns; candidate emits them adjacent) is a list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY tiebreaker that cc1 2.7.2's combine pass actively prevents the C source from accessing — every literal-equivalent constant form (decomposed shift-OR, ~complement, named intermediates, typed constants, NOT-FC000000) folds before the constant materializer's split-vs-adjacent decision. The chain-extender lever family that CAN influence INSN_PRIORITY at the RTL level is FORBIDDEN per [[no-new-park-categories]] § cheats-by-any-spelling + [[global-label-drift-sibling-cheat]] § FORBIDDEN (combine-foldable chains have no semantic purpose). Same family of structural ceiling as cpu_side_move_dir_4 (10 sessions + 16,800-iter clean-target permuter plateau at sandbox class 6 / register-rename diffs at matched insn count). USER POLICY CALL NEEDED: (a) accept that func_8007CBB0 stays INCOMPLETE indefinitely under the asmfix replace_with_asmfile bridge, with the score-52 candidate documented but not deployed — same status as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec / saEft00Add and the other documented sched.c-class plateaus; (b) authorize a permuter-budget round (cpu_side_move_dir_4 burned ~3 wall-clock hours / 16,800 iters in session 10 to confirm the plateau; CBB0's would likely follow the same arc) to formalize the negative result; (c) authorize broadening the canonical-asm gate to admit this list-scheduler-class plateau as canonical-body asm (would require user judgment per [[hand-coded-asm-recognition]] — the function does NOT show hand-coded signals like GTE ops, hardcoded $N registers, or annotations in target.s, so this likely fails the bar). Recommendation: option (a). The function is functionally complete (bridge holds oracle bytes), the structural analysis is exhaustively documented for any future toolchain change that might unlock the residual, and dispatching N similar-class sessions on the cluster (8007C7A0, 8007C86C, 8007C97C, 8007B844, etc., all parked with similar evidence ledgers) wastes the worker budget. Option (b) would benefit from a single shared permuter sweep across the cluster after a future engine improvement (e.g., a SOTN-precedent rule update that legitimizes a new chain-extender class).

Recipe: (1) probe cc1's current label sequence for func_8007CE0C via `bash tmp/probe_func_labels.sh func_8007CE0C src/display.c`; (2) identify slot # for each of .L152/.L154/.L156/.L158/.L159/.L160/.L174; (3) rewrite each splice rule replacing literal labels with `{lbl#N}` placeholders; (4) verify-oracle --rebuild — bytes don't change, confirms migration is correct; (5) NOW the candidate body for CBB0 can be deployed and measured without breaking CE0C. ALSO: the sandbox-noscoring gotcha for display.c (sibling index-based reorder rule breaking on cheat-asm strip) is a separate meta-blocker; the C86C round-3 worker's `--keep-cheat-asm` workaround should work for CBB0 too (this function's HEAD body is the stub-with-bridge form; the candidate has no cheat-asm). If both infrastructure migrations land, THEN execute the original directed-permuter hypothesis from a CLEAN single-function offset-0 target. The candidate body changes cc1's global .L label allocation count for display.c, breaking func_8007CE0C's 3 splice rules (regfix.txt:4208, 4210, 4212) which reference absolute global labels. This is a NEW round-3 finding: the structural problem is not just the 52 list-scheduler floor — it is also a deployment-time meta-blocker that requires sibling-rule {lbl#N} migration first. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

Cheat-disabled build of display.c is missing func_8007CBB0 (pipeline likely truncated by a sibling index-based reorder rule after cheat-asm strip).' (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: Named-intermediates for ot_hdr and sign_E1 in big-packet block
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). `u32 ot_hdr = ((u32)p187C & 0xFFFFFF) | 0x08000000; u32 sign_E1 = ((arg1>>31)<<10) | 0xE1000000;` declared at top of big-packet block; D_800F1858 = ot_hdr; D_800F186C = (*D_8009BF48 & 0x7FF) | sign_E1. GCC's combine pass folds single-use named intermediates back to their inline expressions before RTL pseudo numbering — no LUID effect, no scheduling change. (Workflow round 4, 2026-06-04.)

No floor reduction (still 52). Tried to coerce GCC's constant materialization to split lui from ori (target has them 36 insns apart; candidate emits adjacent). Combine canonicalizes the constant before reaching the MIPS expander; emitted bytes identical to writing 0x03FFFFFF directly. (Workflow round 4, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Hoisted BF48 deref via s32 ot_link = *D_8009BF48 at top of big-packet block
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 52 -> 65. Hypothesis was that hoisting the BF48 load earlier would influence the list-scheduler's INSN_PRIORITY calculation for F186C's store. Instead, the early load pollutes the scheduling space — the dereferenced value sits in a register too long, GCC cascades and emits more spilling / wider register lifetimes. Confirms that the BF48 load's position is structurally bound to its consumer's scheduling; moving it does not flip the constant-materialization split. (Workflow round 4, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Move *p187C = 0x03FFFFFF; BEFORE D_800F1874 store (between D_800F186C and D_800F1874 in source)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 52 -> 53. Symmetric to the prior 'AFTER D_800F1878' test (which scored 54). The target's actual store order has 187C between 1874 and 1878; moving 187C earlier in source breaks that source-to-emitted store correspondence without giving the scheduler new freedom on the constant materialization split. Confirms the source store order for the trailing-cluster (1874, 187C, 1878) is structurally pinned by the existing candidate form. (Workflow round 5, 2026-06-04.)

No floor reduction (still 52). Hypothesis was that writing the constant in shift-OR form would map directly onto GCC's lui+ori RTL expansion, biasing combine to keep them as discrete operations that the list-scheduler could then split. Result: combine's constant canonicalization runs BEFORE the constant-materializer's split-vs-adjacent decision; the shift-OR form folds to the literal 0x03FFFFFF and emits the adjacent lui+ori pair the candidate already has. Confirms that no literal-equivalent source spelling of 0x03FFFFFF reaches GCC's constant materializer in a non-canonicalized form. (Workflow round 5, 2026-06-04.)

structural_evidence.build_insns_match: 151 candidate == 151 target — EXACT match. Structural decomp is end-to-end correct.
structural_evidence.register_match: arg0 in $t0, arg1 in $t1, $s0 holds 0xE3000000, $s1 holds 0xE5000000 — all match target.
structural_evidence.small_packet_stores: 6 stores in order F1858/F185C/F1864/F1860/F1868/F186C — matches target.
structural_evidence.clamp_shape: Both clamps emit `lh; nop; addu v1,v0,zero; addiu v0,v0,-1; slt; bnez` (matches target shape, including the 'save BE78 into $v1' trick).
structural_evidence.residual: 52 masked diffs = pure list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the constant-materialization/load chain interleaving.
